title: Stand up Workstreams/.agents/
outcome: Stood up Workstreams/.agents/ as the operator-facing navigation layer. READMEs for the home + all four subdirs (profiles, tools, skills, graphs) document the contract and the division of responsibility: Ontarch descriptors/policies are the machine-routing authority, .agents/ is the human/agent navigation layer. ontarch-sync now also generates .agents/tools/local-toolkit.yml from the Panoply manifest + tools.json, classifying every tool into one mutually-exclusive bucket: present (installed), missing (absent default), candidate (optional not installed), deprecated (taxonomy slot). .agents/.gitignore keeps the generated local-toolkit.yml out of version control while the README contracts stay tracked. Fixed a path bug in lib/common.sh: WS_ROOT is four levels above the wfos workspace (Workstreams), not three (Build); the stray Build/.agents created by the bug was removed.
validation: GATE moon run ontarch:sync PASS — local-toolkit.yml written to .agents/tools/ with 44 classified tools (present 42, missing 0, candidate 2 [aube, qmd], deprecated 0). YAML structurally valid. moon run ontarch:validate still PASS (3/3). shellcheck --severity=warning CLEAN.
decisions: local-toolkit.yml is generated (host-specific) and gitignored; the tracked artifact is tools/README.md documenting the present/missing/candidate/deprecated taxonomy
each tool is classified into exactly one bucket (installed -> present; absent default -> missing; optional absent -> candidate) so the lists do not overlap
the READMEs are authored (tracked) navigation contracts; .agents stays data, not code — generation/validation live in Ontarch
next: E04.S4 — policy & graph descriptors (no-agent-git-push + generated graph)
token_note: one directory (.agents/) + one generated file answers 'what profiles/tools/skills exist and which tools are present/missing' without scanning the manifest or probing the host
